description: A small village tavern in the Sundered Reach. The keeper polishes mugs at the bar; two villagers nurse drinks at the tables, eyes on the door. Small enough that no conversation goes unheard.
objective: Speak with Bram and listen for what the villagers are whispering about.
customIntroduction: You duck under the lintel of the Sparrow's Nest. The room is barely a dozen paces across — a short bar along the north wall, a pair of tables to either side, a low fire muttering in the grate. The shutters are drawn against the cart-paths beyond. Bram Holdfast looks up from a mug, then back down. Two villagers — one at each table — glance at you and quickly look away.
customContext: Small-tavern social encounter in the Sundered Reach. The player enters from the south door. Bram Holdfast keeps the short bar along the north wall — close enough to overhear the patrons. Two local villagers (commoners faction) sit at the single-tile tables on either side of the room. All NPCs are neutral; combat is not expected. The AIGM should make the room feel intimate and watchful — every clink of cutlery is audible, the villagers are aware of one another, and nobody talks openly about the New Empire, the Concordat, or the White Capes within earshot of the door. Bram is the primary information source and speaks plainly; the villagers offer guarded scraps of rumour. ESCALATION: If the player turns violent, Bram shouts for help — call spawn_enemy twice with defId "bandit" (or another available hostile def) near the door, then set Bram and any present villager to enemy disposition so they defend the establishment together.
